task_completion.description: The draw.io application is open with the starter file 'mapk_pathway.drawio' loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io (diagrams.net) GUI is visible on screen, and the window title or workspace indicates that 'mapk_pathway.drawio' is the active file.

task_completion.description: The diagram contains shapes for all 10 required signaling molecules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Shapes containing the text for EGF, EGFR, GRB2, SOS, RAS, RAF, MEK, ERK, ELK1, and MYC are visible in the drawing workspace.

task_completion.description: The molecules are connected by directed arrows showing the correct signal flow cascade.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Directed arrows connect the molecules in the correct sequence: EGF->EGFR->GRB2->SOS->RAS->RAF->MEK->ERK, with ERK branching to both ELK1 and MYC.

task_completion.description: The diagram follows a color-coding scheme and includes a legend.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The molecule shapes have distinct fill colors applied to them, and there is a separate 'Legend' box or section on the canvas explaining the color scheme.

task_completion.description: The final diagram is exported as a PNG to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing 'ls ~/Diagrams/exports/' with 'mapk_pathway.png' listed, a file manager showing the file in the correct folder, or a draw.io export dialog confirming the save location as '/home/ga/Diagrams/exports/mapk_pathway.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with draw.io menus, dragging shapes from the sidebar, or using the color palette, rather than just running a script in the terminal to generate the file.

integrity.description: The exported PNG matches the diagram built in the workspace.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual layout, colors, and shapes of the diagram in the draw.io workspace match the final exported image or the image preview.

integrity.description: The agent did not download a pre-made MAPK pathway image from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no web browser open to Google Images or Wikipedia downloading a pathway diagram to bypass the drawing task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The diagram must contain exactly 10 signaling molecules: EGF, EGFR, GRB2, SOS, RAS, RAF, MEK, ERK, ELK1, MYC. The correct signal flow requires 9 directed arrows: EGF->EGFR, EGFR->GRB2, GRB2->SOS, SOS->RAS, RAS->RAF, RAF->MEK, MEK->ERK, ERK->ELK1, and ERK->MYC. Cellular compartments (based on the starter file layout): EGF is extracellular, EGFR is on the membrane, GRB2/SOS/RAS/RAF/MEK/ERK are in the cytoplasm, and ELK1/MYC are in the nucleus. The final export must be located at '/home/ga/Diagrams/exports/mapk_pathway.png'.
